title: tephrosia striata
intro: Tephrosia striata, also known as Striped Tephrosia or Striped Pea Tree, is a perennial legume native to Africa, characterized by its distinctive striped stems and clusters of small, white or pink flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. Tephrosia striata has been reported to have various health benefits, such as lowering blood pressure, reducing fever, and treating digestive issues. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and stems can be used to make teas, infusions, and topical creams, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
